Name: Flow Cytometry
Description: Flow cytometry is a laser-based, high-throughput single-cell analysis technique used to simultaneously measure multiple physical and fluorescent characteristics of cells or particles in suspension. This assay quantifies cell populations based on parameters such as cell size (forward scatter), granularity (side scatter), and fluorescence intensity of labeled surface or intracellular markers, enabling immunophenotyping, cell cycle analysis, viability assessment, and functional studies. Samples are typically prepared as single-cell suspensions, stained with fluorochrome-conjugated antibodies or dyes, and acquired on a flow cytometer, with subsequent data analysis performed using dedicated software (e.g., FlowJo, FCS Express) to gate and enumerate distinct cellular subpopulations. The "Data Linked" designation indicates that the assay results are associated with externally referenced or linked datasets, facilitating integrated analysis across experimental cohorts or studies.
Tags: flow cytometry, FACS, immunophenotyping, cell surface marker staining, fluorescence cytometry, multi-parameter cell analysis, cell population profiling
Required Parent Sample Types: TIS or CEX or CEL, AB or ABP
Protocols_Phrases: strip beginning and end of the protocols, give it phrases and not IDs

Name: Gene Expression Analysis
Description:
Gene expression analysis converts raw sequencing reads into quantified transcript abundance. It involves aligning reads to a reference genome or transcriptome, followed by normalization and quality control.
Input: Sequencing file (e.g., FASTQ / D.SEQ) Output: Processed expression matrices and summary statistics.
Tags: RNA-seq analysis, differential expression analysis, DESeq2, edgeR, count matrix processing, transcriptomics analysis, STAR kallisto salmon

Name: RNA Extraction
Description: RNA extraction is a laboratory procedure used to isolate and purify total RNA from biological samples, serving as a foundational step for downstream transcriptomic analyses such as RNA sequencing, RT-PCR, or microarray profiling. The process typically involves cell lysis, denaturation of protein-RNA complexes, and purification using methods such as phenol-chloroform extraction, silica column-based kits, or magnetic bead-based approaches to yield high-quality RNA free of DNA, protein, and other contaminants. Key quality metrics assessed during and after extraction include RNA integrity number (RIN), A260/A280 and A260/A230 absorbance ratios, and RNA concentration, which are commonly measured using instruments such as a NanoDrop spectrophotometer or Bioanalyzer. Proper sample handling, including maintenance of an RNase-free environment and cold-chain integrity, is critical to preserving RNA quality and ensuring reproducibility across experimental conditions.
Tags: RNA isolation, RNA purification, total RNA extraction, Trizol extraction, column RNA extraction, RNA integrity RIN, RNA isolation kit

Name: Single Cell Sequencing
Description:
Single cell sequencing generates sequencing data from individual cells within a mixed population. The assay involves capturing or barcoding single cells, amplifying nucleic acids, and sequencing the resulting libraries.
Input: Single-cell DNA/RNA sample Output: Sequencing file (e.g., FASTQ / D.SEQ) containing cell-resolved read data.
Tags: scRNA-seq, 10x Genomics, single cell sequencing, droplet-based sequencing, cell barcoding, scATAC-seq, single cell NGS

Name: Tissue Collection
Description: This assay captures metadata associated with tissue collection procedures, documenting critical provenance and contextual information for biological specimens obtained from study subjects. It records standardized parameters such as tissue type, anatomical source, collection method, preservation conditions, and relevant donor or sample identifiers to ensure traceability and reproducibility across downstream experimental workflows. This metadata serves as a foundational record linking collected tissues to subsequent analytical assays, enabling proper data integration, quality control, and interpretation of results within the broader research study.
Tags: tissue harvest, biopsy collection, tissue sampling, specimen collection, organ harvest, tissue extraction, sample collection

Name: Absorption and Emission Spectroscopy
Description:
Absorption and emission spectroscopy measures the interaction between light and a sample to assess molecular properties. The assay detects absorbed or emitted light at specific wavelengths to characterize compounds or biomolecules.
Input: Chemical or biological sample Output: Spectral data file containing absorbance or emission intensity profiles.
Tags: UV-Vis spectroscopy, fluorescence spectroscopy, emission spectroscopy, absorption spectroscopy, optical spectroscopy, spectrophotometry, excitation emission measurement

Name: Absorption and Emission Spectroscopy Analysis
Description:
Absorption and emission spectroscopy analysis processes raw spectral data to identify and quantify molecular features. It involves baseline correction, peak fitting, and normalization to generate interpretable spectra.
Input: Spectral data file Output: Processed absorption or emission profiles and quantitative summaries.
Tags: spectral analysis, absorbance analysis, emission analysis, UV-Vis analysis, fluorescence data processing, peak fitting, baseline correction

Name: Agglutination Assay
Description: An agglutination assay is an immunological technique that detects and quantifies antibody-antigen interactions through visible particle clumping (agglutination). The assay works by mixing serial dilutions of antiserum with target particles — which may be bacteria, red blood cells coated with antigens, or latex beads conjugated to specific analytes. After incubation, agglutination is assessed visually or by turbidimetric measurement: agglutinated samples show dispersed mats or granular clumping, while non-agglutinated samples form tight pellets. Results are typically reported as agglutination titers (the highest dilution producing visible clumping) scored on a scale from +1 (weak) to +4 (strong). Agglutination assays are used to quantify antibody titers, identify immune responses to bacterial or viral antigens, and serve as diagnostic tools in immunology and microbiology. Reference: https://cmdr.ubc.ca/bobh/method/agglutination-assays/
Tags: agglutination, hemagglutination, latex agglutination, antigen-antibody clumping, particle agglutination, serological titer, turbidimetric assay

Name: Analyzed Spatial Proteomics
Description: Spatial proteomics is a methodology that maps the distribution, abundance, and localization of proteins within tissue sections or cellular compartments while preserving spatial context. This assay typically employs imaging-based platforms such as multiplex immunofluorescence, imaging mass cytometry (IMC), or Digital Spatial Profiling (DSP) to simultaneously detect and quantify multiple protein targets at defined spatial coordinates. Sample preparation generally involves formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) or fresh-frozen tissue sections subjected to antigen retrieval, antibody staining or metal-conjugated probe hybridization, followed by high-resolution image acquisition and computational analysis to deconvolve protein expression patterns across tissue microenvironments. The resulting spatially resolved proteomic data enables characterization of cell phenotypes, cell-cell interactions, and tissue architecture in the context of disease states or biological processes.
Tags: spatial proteomics analysis, Digital Spatial Profiling, DSP analysis, multiplex immunofluorescence analysis, IMC analysis, imaging mass cytometry analysis, spatial protein mapping

Name: Antibody Treatment
Description:
An antibody treatment assay exposes biological samples or model organisms to specific antibodies to induce or modulate a response. It is used to study therapeutic efficacy, immune regulation, or target engagement.
Input: Biological system or culture Output: Treated sample or response dataset reflecting antibody effects.

Input: Antibody-treated target cells or particles Output: Complement deposition data file or fluorescence readout.
Tags: ADCD, complement deposition assay, C3 deposition, complement activation, opsonization complement, antibody complement fixing

Name: Antibody Dependent Functional Profiling
Description:
Antibody dependent functional profiling evaluates multiple immune effector activities triggered by antibodies. The assay integrates data from complement activation, phagocytosis, and cytotoxicity measurements to define immune functionality.
Input: Antibody-containing biological sample Output: Functional profiling data representing combined antibody responses.
Tags: ADFP, antibody effector function, systems serology, functional immune profiling, Fc effector profiling, humoral immune function

Antibody functional profiling analysis processes raw functional data to quantify and compare antibody responses. It includes normalization, clustering, and visualization to identify immune activity patterns.
Input: Raw antibody functional profiling data Output: Processed immune response metrics and comparative profiles.
Tags: antibody profiling analysis, systems serology analysis, Fc function analysis, immune effector function analysis

Name: Antibody Titer

An antibody titer assay determines the concentration of antibodies in a sample through serial dilution and detection. The process measures the dilution at which a detectable signal is maintained to assess immune strength.
Input: Serum or antibody-containing sample Output: Quantitative antibody titer value.
Tags: antibody titer, serology titer, ELISA titer, binding titer, endpoint dilution titer, antibody quantification, immunoglobulin measurement

Name: Antibody Titer Analysis
Description:
Antibody titer analysis interprets and visualizes quantitative results from antibody titration assays. It calculates endpoint values and generates comparative summaries across samples or timepoints.

Tags: AFM, atomic force microscopy, nanoscale imaging, surface topography, force spectroscopy, nanoindentation, cantilever scanning

A bacterial challenge assay exposes biological samples or model organisms to a defined bacterial strain. It is used to study infection dynamics, host-pathogen interactions, or immune response under controlled conditions.

Name: Bulk DNA Sequencing
Description: Bulk DNA sequencing is a high-throughput genomic assay that measures DNA sequence information across a population of cells simultaneously, capturing the aggregate genetic landscape of a sample without single-cell resolution. This method typically involves extraction and fragmentation of genomic DNA, library preparation (including adapter ligation and optional target enrichment or amplification), and sequencing on next-generation sequencing (NGS) platforms such as Illumina instruments. The resulting sequence reads are aligned to a reference genome and analyzed bioinformatically to identify genetic variants, structural alterations, copy number changes, or other genomic features present within the bulk cell population.
Tags: WGS, whole genome sequencing, WES, whole exome sequencing, bulk sequencing, population-level sequencing, NGS DNA sequencing

Name: Cancer Cell Extravasation Analysis
Description: This assay quantifies the extravasation capacity of cancer cells, measuring their ability to migrate across endothelial barriers as a model of hematogenous metastasis. Using linked experimental data, the assay tracks the transmigration of cancer cells through endothelial monolayers under controlled conditions, capturing key parameters such as the number and rate of cells that successfully breach the vascular barrier. Results are analyzed to provide quantitative metrics of invasive potential, supporting the study of metastatic mechanisms and the evaluation of potential anti-metastatic interventions.
Tags: extravasation analysis, metastasis assay analysis, transendothelial migration analysis, vascular barrier analysis, invasion analysis, tumor cell motility analysis

Name: Cell Line Creation
Description: Cell line creation encompasses the processes used to establish stable or transient cell populations derived from primary tissues, immortalization procedures, or genetic engineering strategies for use in downstream biological assays. This workflow typically involves transfection or transduction of target constructs, selection under appropriate antibiotic or fluorescent marker pressure, single-cell cloning, and expansion of confirmed clones. Resulting cell lines are characterized for expression of relevant transgenes or endogenous targets, genomic integrity, and phenotypic stability prior to use in functional studies. These engineered or adapted cell populations serve as reproducible biological reagents for a broad range of in vitro experimental applications.
Tags: cell line establishment, cell immortalization, stable line generation, transfection, clonal selection, cell engineering

Name: Cell Lysate
Description:
Cell lysis breaks open cells to release proteins, nucleic acids, and other intracellular components. The lysate is used for downstream biochemical or molecular assays.
Input: Cultured or isolated cells Output: Cell lysate containing extracted biomolecules.
Tags: cell lysis, lysate preparation, cellular homogenate, intracellular protein release, mechanical lysis, detergent lysis

Name: Cell Sorting
Description: Cell sorting is a flow cytometry-based assay used to physically separate heterogeneous cell populations into distinct subpopulations based on specific cell surface or intracellular markers, enabling downstream functional, molecular, or phenotypic analyses. Cells are labeled with fluorescently conjugated antibodies or dyes, interrogated by laser excitation, and individually sorted into collection vessels using electrostatic deflection or microfluidic mechanisms according to predefined gating strategies. This dataset represents cell sorting results that are linked to associated experimental or clinical data, facilitating integrative analyses across multiple assay types or study endpoints.
Tags: FACS, fluorescence-activated cell sorting, cell sorting, flow-based cell separation, electrostatic deflection sorting, cell fractionation

Name: Cell Viability
Description:
A cell viability assay measures the proportion of living cells within a population following treatment or culture. It assesses metabolic activity, membrane integrity, or dye exclusion to determine survival rate.
Input: Cultured or treated cells Output: Viability data or quantitative survival measurement.
Tags: viability assay, cytotoxicity assay, cell survival, cell death assessment, metabolic activity assay, proliferation assay

Chemical simulations use computational modeling to predict molecular behavior and interactions. The process applies physical and chemical principles to estimate reaction outcomes or binding affinities.
Input: Molecular or compound parameters Output: Simulated dataset describing chemical interactions or energy states.
Tags: computational chemistry, molecular simulation, in silico chemistry, DFT simulation, molecular dynamics, reaction energy calculation

Name: Chemical Synthesis
Description:
Chemical synthesis combines reagents under controlled conditions to produce specific compounds or molecules. The assay focuses on generating pure and reproducible products for experimental or analytical use.
Input: Chemical precursors or reagents Output: Synthesized compound or purified chemical sample.
Tags: organic synthesis, compound synthesis, chemical preparation, drug synthesis, synthetic chemistry, reaction product, reagent preparation

Name: Chromatin Sequencing Analysis
Description: Chromatin sequencing analysis is a genomic assay that interrogates the structural and regulatory landscape of chromatin by sequencing DNA associated with specific chromatin states, protein binding sites, or accessibility regions. This approach typically employs next-generation sequencing (NGS) technologies following chromatin preparation steps such as immunoprecipitation (ChIP-seq), transposase-based accessibility profiling (ATAC-seq), or nuclease digestion (MNase-seq or FAIRE-seq), depending on the specific application. The resulting sequencing data are computationally processed through alignment, peak calling, and downstream statistical analyses to identify genomic loci associated with transcription factor binding, histone modifications, or open chromatin regions. This assay provides high-resolution, genome-wide maps of chromatin organization that are linked to functional regulatory elements and gene expression data.
Tags: ATAC-seq analysis, ChIP-seq analysis, chromatin accessibility analysis, epigenomics analysis, histone modification analysis, open chromatin analysis, regulatory element mapping

Name: CITESEQ Analysis
Description:
CITE-seq analysis processes multimodal single-cell data combining RNA sequencing and antibody-derived tags. It integrates transcriptomic and surface protein profiles to classify cell types and states.
Input: Raw CITE-seq data Output: Processed expression matrices and integrated cell-type annotations.
Tags: CITE-seq analysis, multimodal single cell analysis, ADT RNA integration, surface protein expression analysis, multiomics single cell

Name: Clot Modeling Analysis
Description: This assay employs computational clot modeling analysis to characterize the mechanical and structural properties of blood clots by integrating linked experimental datasets. The approach combines empirical measurements with mathematical or simulation-based frameworks to quantify parameters such as clot stiffness, fibrin network architecture, viscoelastic behavior, and thrombus formation kinetics. Data linkage enables cross-referencing of multiple input variables — which may include rheological, imaging, or biochemical measurements — to generate holistic models of clot development and stability. Results support the investigation of coagulation disorders, therapeutic interventions, and hemostatic function across diverse patient populations or experimental conditions.
Tags: clot analysis, coagulation modeling, fibrin network analysis, blood clot mechanics, thrombus analysis, viscoelastic clot analysis

Name: Comet Chip
Description:
A comet chip assay measures DNA strand breaks by electrophoretic separation of nuclear material within microfabricated wells. It visualizes DNA migration patterns to assess genotoxicity or repair efficiency.
Input: Treated or control cell samples Output: Comet chip imaging data showing DNA damage profiles.
Tags: comet assay, single cell gel electrophoresis, DNA damage assay, genotoxicity assay, DNA strand break measurement, tail moment
Required Parent Sample Types: TIS or CEL or MUS or CHM

Name: Comet Chip Analysis
Description:
Comet chip analysis quantifies DNA damage from imaging data collected in comet chip assays. It calculates tail length, moment, and intensity metrics to compare sample damage levels.
Input: Raw comet chip imaging data Output: Processed DNA damage metrics and visual summaries.
Tags: comet chip analysis, DNA damage quantification, tail length analysis, tail moment analysis, comet image analysis, genotoxicity analysis

Name: Copy Number Variation Analysis
Description: Copy Number Variation (CNV) analysis is performed using bulk DNA sequencing to detect and quantify genomic amplifications, deletions, and other structural alterations across the genome. Genomic DNA is extracted from bulk cell populations, subjected to library preparation, and sequenced using high-throughput next-generation sequencing (NGS) platforms. Sequencing read depth and coverage uniformity are analyzed computationally to identify regions of aberrant copy number relative to a reference, enabling the characterization of somatic or germline CNVs at a genome-wide scale.
Tags: CNV analysis, copy number alteration, CNA, genomic amplification deletion, structural variant, somatic copy number, genome-wide copy number

Name: Crystallography
Description:
Crystallography determines molecular or atomic structures by analyzing diffraction patterns from crystallized samples. The assay provides 3D models that reveal spatial arrangement and bonding.
Input: Crystallized biological or chemical sample Output: Diffraction data and derived structural model files.
Tags: X-ray crystallography, crystal structure determination, diffraction analysis, single crystal XRD, Bragg diffraction, 3D molecular structure

Name: CyCIF
Description:
Cyclic immunofluorescence (CyCIF) sequentially stains and images tissue sections to measure multiple protein markers. The assay captures high-resolution spatial patterns of protein expression.
Input: Tissue section or fixed cell sample Output: Multiplexed fluorescence imaging data.
Tags: cyclic immunofluorescence, CyCIF, multiplexed tissue imaging, iterative IF staining, t-CyCIF, tissue cyclic staining

Name: cyCIF Analysis
Description: Cyclic immunofluorescence (CyCIF) is a multiplexed imaging assay that enables sequential, quantitative detection of numerous protein targets within intact tissue sections or cultured cells through iterative rounds of fluorescent antibody staining, imaging, and signal inactivation. Each cycle involves the application of a panel of fluorophore-conjugated primary or secondary antibodies, followed by wide-field or confocal fluorescence microscopy imaging, and subsequent chemical bleaching or inactivation of fluorescent signals to permit successive rounds of staining on the same sample. Raw multichannel images from each cycle are computationally registered, stitched, and overlaid to generate a single high-dimensional dataset, which is then subjected to cell segmentation and single-cell feature extraction to quantify protein expression, co-expression patterns, and spatial relationships at the single-cell level. This approach allows simultaneous profiling of cell identity, functional state, and tissue architecture across dozens of markers within a spatially resolved context.
Tags: cyCIF analysis, cyclic immunofluorescence analysis, multiplexed imaging analysis, iterative IF analysis, spatial protein quantification, tissue IF cycling

Name: CyTOF
Description: Cytometry by Time of Flight (CyTOF) is a mass spectrometry-based single-cell analysis platform that enables simultaneous quantification of dozens of protein markers at the single-cell level by labeling antibodies with isotopically pure heavy metal tags rather than fluorescent dyes, thereby eliminating spectral overlap limitations inherent to conventional flow cytometry. Individual cells are introduced into an inductively coupled plasma ion source, vaporized and ionized, and the resulting metal isotope signals are detected and quantified by a time-of-flight mass spectrometer, providing precise measurements of marker expression across large cell populations. This technology is particularly suited for deep immunophenotyping, functional protein analysis, and characterization of complex cellular heterogeneity in samples such as peripheral blood, bone marrow, and tumor-infiltrating immune cells, supporting high-dimensional data analysis workflows including dimensionality reduction and unsupervised clustering approaches.
Tags: mass cytometry, CyTOF, metal-labeled antibody cytometry, high-dimensional cytometry, single cell protein profiling, HDCyto, time-of-flight mass spec cytometry

Name: CyTOF Analysis
Description: Mass cytometry (CyTOF) is a single-cell proteomic technology that enables simultaneous quantification of dozens of protein markers per cell using heavy metal isotope-conjugated antibodies detected via inductively coupled plasma time-of-flight mass spectrometry. This assay measures the expression of multiple surface and intracellular proteins at the single-cell level, enabling high-dimensional immunophenotyping and functional characterization of complex cell populations without the spectral overlap limitations of conventional fluorescence-based flow cytometry. Data generated from CyTOF analysis is typically processed through normalization and dimensionality reduction pipelines (e.g., viSNE, UMAP, or PCA) to enable visualization and clustering of distinct cell subsets across large, multiplexed datasets.
Tags: CyTOF analysis, mass cytometry analysis, high-dimensional clustering, viSNE, UMAP mass cytometry, metal-tag cytometry analysis, single cell protein analysis

Name: Microfluidic Device Creation
Description:
Microfluidic device creation fabricates miniature fluidic systems designed to control and analyze small sample volumes. The process involves patterning channels and chambers into materials such as PDMS or glass.
Input: Design pattern or mold Output: Functional microfluidic device ready for experimental use.

Tags: microfluidic device imaging, chip imaging, OOC imaging, organ-on-chip microscopy, lab-on-chip visualization

Name: DNA Extraction

This assay encompasses the collection of relevant metadata associated with the extraction process, including sample identifiers, source material type, extraction method or kit utilized, instrument parameters, operator information, and quality control metrics such as DNA yield and purity (e.g., A260/A280 ratios). Standardized metadata capture ensures traceability, reproducibility, and integrity of extracted nucleic acid samples across subsequent applications such as sequencing, genotyping, or PCR-based assays.
Tags: DNA isolation, genomic DNA extraction, gDNA extraction, nucleic acid extraction, DNA purification, QIAamp extraction

Electrochemical sensor testing measures electrical responses from sensors exposed to defined analytes. The assay characterizes sensitivity, stability, and signal-to-noise performance under experimental conditions.

Input: Prepared chemical or biological sample Output: EPR spectral data representing paramagnetic properties.
Tags: EPR, ESR, electron spin resonance, paramagnetic spectroscopy, free radical detection, unpaired electron measurement

Name: ELISA
Description: This assay employs an enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) methodology to quantitatively detect and measure specific analytes (such as proteins, antibodies, or other biomolecules) in biological samples. The technique utilizes antibody-antigen interactions coupled with enzymatic signal amplification to generate colorimetric or fluorometric readouts proportional to analyte concentration. Sample preparation, incubation conditions, wash steps, and detection reagents are performed according to standardized protocols, with absorbance or fluorescence values measured using a microplate reader and quantified against a calibration curve. Data are linked to associated experimental records to facilitate integrated downstream analysis and result interpretation.
Tags: enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, ELISA, sandwich ELISA, plate-based immunoassay, OD measurement, absorbance immunoassay, microplate reader assay

Name: Fc Receptor Binding Assay
Description: This assay evaluates the binding affinity and interaction of test compounds or biological molecules (such as antibodies or Fc-fusion proteins) to Fc receptors (FcRs), which are critical mediators of immune effector functions including antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and phagocytosis. Binding interactions are quantified using established biochemical or biophysical detection methodologies, enabling characterization of Fc-FcR engagement relevant to the functional activity of immunoglobulins or Fc-containing therapeutics. Results are linked to associated experimental datasets, facilitating integrated analysis of structure-activity relationships and supporting the development and optimization of antibody-based therapeutics.
Tags: Fc receptor binding assay, FcR binding, Fc-gamma receptor assay, FCRB assay, antibody effector function assay, receptor affinity measurement

Name: FC Receptor Binding Assay Analysis

Input: Deployed water sensor Output: Environmental dataset containing sensor measurements over time.
Tags: water sensor run, field sensor deployment, in-situ water sensing, environmental water monitoring, aquatic sensor data

Name: Flow Cytometry Analysis
Description: Flow cytometry is employed to quantitatively measure cell surface and/or intracellular markers, enabling characterization of cell populations based on fluorescence intensity and light scatter properties. This assay utilizes a flow cytometer to analyze individual cells in suspension, providing multi-parametric data on cell phenotype, viability, activation state, or other biological attributes as defined by the experimental panel. Samples are processed, stained with fluorescently labeled antibodies or dyes, and acquired on the instrument, with downstream analysis performed using dedicated gating strategies to identify and quantify cell subsets of interest.
Tags: flow cytometry analysis, FACS analysis, gating analysis, cell population analysis, FlowJo analysis, FCS Express, immunophenotyping analysis

Name: Fourier Transform Infrared Spectroscopy
Description:
Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR) measures absorption of infrared light to characterize molecular vibrations. The assay provides information on chemical composition and structural features.

Name: Gel Permeation Chromatography
Description:
Gel permeation chromatography (GPC) separates molecules based on size using a porous gel matrix. The assay measures elution times to determine molecular weight distribution.
Input: Polymer or macromolecular sample Output: Chromatography trace showing molecular size distribution.
Tags: GPC, size exclusion chromatography, SEC, molecular weight distribution, polymer size separation, chromatography fractionation

Name: Genome Alignment
Description: This assay involves the computational alignment of sequencing reads to a reference genome, enabling the mapping of raw sequence data to genomic coordinates for downstream variant calling, expression quantification, or structural analysis. Sequencing data files (e.g., FASTQ) are processed using standard bioinformatic alignment tools (such as BWA, STAR, or Bowtie2) to produce aligned output files (e.g., BAM/SAM format), which are subsequently sorted, indexed, and subjected to quality control assessment. This linked-data implementation associates the resulting alignment files directly with corresponding sample metadata and upstream sequencing assay records, facilitating integrated, reproducible genomic analyses within a structured data management framework.
Tags: read alignment, sequence mapping, reference genome alignment, BWA STAR bowtie, BAM generation, read mapping, NGS alignment

Name: Glycosylation Assay
Description: This assay characterizes the glycosylation profile of biological samples by analyzing the composition, structure, and abundance of glycan moieties attached to proteins or other biomolecules. Standard glycosylation analysis workflows typically involve sample preparation steps such as enzymatic release of N-linked glycans (e.g., via PNGase F treatment), followed by labeling or derivatization and separation using chromatographic or electrophoretic techniques (such as HPLC, UHPLC, or capillary electrophoresis) coupled with fluorescence or mass spectrometric detection. Data are linked to associated experimental records to enable integrated downstream analysis, including quantification of individual glycan species, calculation of derived glycosylation traits, and assessment of batch or sample quality. Results support characterization of glycan heterogeneity and may be used to monitor biological processes, product quality attributes, or disease-associated glycosylation changes.
Tags: glycan analysis, sugar modification analysis, N-glycan profiling, O-glycan analysis, antibody glycosylation, glycoproteomics, carbohydrate characterization

Name: GPT Assay
Description:
A GPT (glutamate pyruvate transaminase) assay measures enzyme activity involved in amino acid metabolism. It monitors substrate conversion rates to assess liver function or cellular health.
Input: Biological or enzyme-containing sample Output: Enzyme activity data or absorbance measurement.
Tags: GPT assay, glutamate pyruvate transaminase, phage efficiency assay, plaque assay, bacterial transaminase, phage titer

Name: High Resolution Mass Spectra
Description:
High-resolution mass spectrometry measures molecular mass with high precision to identify and quantify chemical species. The assay provides detailed profiles of metabolites, peptides, or proteins based on ionized fragments.
Input: Prepared chemical or biological sample Output: High-resolution mass spectral data file containing mass-to-charge ratios.
Tags: HRMS, high-resolution mass spectrometry, accurate mass, molecular formula, Orbitrap, QTOF, exact mass measurement

Name: High Resolution Mass Spectra Analysis
Description:
High-resolution mass spectra analysis processes raw spectral data to identify and quantify molecular components. It performs peak detection, alignment, and annotation to generate compound or peptide identifications.
Input: Raw mass spectrometry data Output: Processed spectra and annotated molecular feature tables.
Tags: HRMS analysis, accurate mass analysis, molecular formula confirmation, high-resolution MS data processing

Name: Histopathology
Description: Histology assays employ tissue preparation and staining techniques to examine the microscopic structure of biological specimens, enabling detection of morphological changes, cellular organization, and pathological features within tissues. Standard workflows typically involve fixation, embedding, sectioning, and application of stains (such as hematoxylin and eosin or specialized histochemical stains), followed by microscopic imaging and qualitative or quantitative analysis. This dataset represents histological findings that have been linked to associated experimental or clinical records, facilitating integrated interpretation of tissue-level observations alongside complementary study data.
Tags: histopathology, H&E staining, tissue staining, pathological examination, tissue morphology, hematoxylin eosin, histological analysis

Name: Histopathology Analysis
Description: Histopathology analysis is a diagnostic and investigative technique that evaluates the microscopic structural and cellular characteristics of tissue samples to identify pathological changes, disease states, or treatment-related effects. Tissue specimens are processed through standard histological workflows including fixation (typically in formalin), paraffin embedding, sectioning, and staining with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) or other specialized stains, followed by examination under light microscopy. Quantitative or qualitative assessments may include evaluation of tissue architecture, cellular morphology, inflammatory infiltration, necrosis, fibrosis, or other histopathological endpoints by trained pathologists or automated image analysis systems.
Tags: histopathology analysis, digital pathology, tissue image analysis, H&E analysis, pathological scoring, slide analysis

Name: Imaging
Description: This imaging assay captures and links visual data to corresponding experimental or sample records, enabling integrated analysis of morphological, spatial, or phenotypic features within a structured data management framework. Image acquisition is performed using standard microscopy or imaging instrumentation, with resulting datasets cross-referenced to associated metadata, experimental conditions, or sample identifiers to facilitate downstream quantitative or qualitative analysis. The linked data architecture supports reproducible retrieval and interpretation of imaging outputs in the context of broader experimental workflows.
Tags: microscopy, imaging assay, optical imaging, fluorescence imaging, confocal imaging, brightfield imaging, image acquisition

Name: Imaging Analysis
Description:
Imaging analysis processes raw image data to extract measurements or visual patterns. It includes segmentation, quantification, and visualization of spatial features.
Input: Raw imaging data Output: Processed image metrics and annotated visualizations.
Tags: image analysis, image processing, image quantification, morphology analysis, segmentation analysis, pixel quantification, computer vision analysis

Name: Immunohistochemistry
Description:
Immunohistochemistry detects specific proteins in tissue sections using antibody-based staining. The assay visualizes antigen distribution to assess expression and localization within a biological context.
Input: Fixed tissue section Output: Stained and imaged tissue slides showing protein localization.
Tags: IHC, immunostaining, antibody tissue staining, protein localization tissue, chromogenic IHC, DAB staining, fluorescent IHC

Name: ImmunoPeptideomics
Description:
Immunopeptideomics identifies peptides presented by MHC molecules using mass spectrometry. The assay characterizes antigen presentation and immune recognition across samples.
Input: Protein or peptide extract Output: Mass spectrometry data profiling peptide-MHC complexes.
Tags: immunopeptideomics, MHC peptidome, HLA ligandome, antigen presentation profiling, neoantigen, immune peptide identification

Name: Intravital Microscopy
Description: Intravital microscopy (IVM) is an advanced imaging technique used to visualize and quantify dynamic biological processes in living animals in real time, including cellular behavior, vascular dynamics, immune cell trafficking, and tissue interactions at the microscopic level. The method employs high-resolution optical microscopy platforms — such as confocal, two-photon, or widefield fluorescence microscopes — coupled with appropriate surgical preparation or optical window chambers to provide direct access to tissues of interest (e.g., tumor microenvironment, lymph nodes, liver, or vasculature). Fluorescently labeled cells, proteins, or dyes are introduced into the living subject, and time-lapse image sequences are acquired to track parameters such as cell migration velocity, vessel permeability, leukocyte rolling and adhesion, or reporter gene expression. Quantitative image analysis is subsequently performed using specialized software to extract spatiotemporal metrics from the acquired datasets.
Tags: intravital imaging, in vivo microscopy, two-photon microscopy, live animal imaging, in vivo fluorescence microscopy

Name: iPSC Culture and Chrondronoid Formation
Description:
This assay differentiates induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) into chondronoid structures for cartilage modeling. It combines controlled culture conditions and differentiation factors to generate tissue-like constructs.
Input: Induced pluripotent stem cells Output: Differentiated chondronoid culture ready for analysis or downstream assays.
Tags: iPSC culture, chondronoid formation, stem cell 3D culture, cartilage organoid, induced pluripotent stem cell culture

Name: Library Prep
Description:
Library preparation converts extracted nucleic acids into sequencing-ready libraries. The process includes fragmentation, adapter ligation, and amplification to generate consistent input for sequencing instruments.
Input: TIS, CEL, DNA or RNA sample Output: Indexed sequencing library suitable for sequencing assays.
Tags: library preparation, sequencing library, adapter ligation, NGS library prep, library construction, sequencing input

Name: Linear Mixed Model
Description: A linear mixed model (LMM) statistical framework applied to linked biomedical datasets to assess associations between variables of interest while accounting for both fixed effects and random effects, such as repeated measures, familial relatedness, or population stratification. This analytical approach enables the estimation of effect sizes, variance components, and statistical significance across complex, hierarchically structured data. Results are derived from integrated or linked data sources, allowing for the joint analysis of phenotypic, genetic, or clinical variables within a unified modeling framework.
Tags: LMM, linear mixed model, mixed effects model, random effects model, REML, repeated measures model, longitudinal statistical model

Name: Live Cell Imaging
Description:
Live cell imaging captures time-resolved microscopy data of living cells. It allows observation of cellular dynamics, migration, and response to stimuli under controlled conditions.
Input: Live cultured cells Output: Time-lapse image series documenting cell behavior.
Tags: live cell imaging, time-lapse microscopy, real-time imaging, in vitro live fluorescence, dynamic cell imaging

Name: Long Read Sequencing
Description:
Long read sequencing produces extended nucleotide sequences that span repetitive or complex genomic regions. The assay uses third-generation platforms to generate highly continuous reads for genome assembly.
Input: DNA library Output: Long read sequencing file (e.g., FASTQ / D.SEQ) containing extended read data.
Tags: Oxford Nanopore, PacBio, long-read sequencing, third-generation sequencing, SMRT, nanopore long reads

Name: Luminex
Description:
A Luminex assay quantifies multiple analytes simultaneously using color-coded microspheres and fluorescence detection. The assay measures cytokines, antibodies, or proteins within a single multiplexed reaction.
Input: Biological sample Output: Multiplex fluorescence data file representing analyte concentrations.
Tags: Luminex, xMAP, multiplex bead assay, cytokine panel, bead-based immunoassay, BioPlex, MAGPIX, MFI measurement

Name: Luminex Analysis
Description:
Luminex analysis processes raw fluorescence data from multiplex assays to quantify target analytes. It applies calibration curves and normalization to compute concentration values for each target.
Input: Raw Luminex fluorescence data Output: Processed concentration tables and summary reports.
Tags: Luminex analysis, multiplex data analysis, MFI analysis, cytokine analysis, bead-based assay analysis, standard curve analysis

Name: MALDI-MSI
Description:
Matrix-Assisted Laser Desorption/Ionization Mass Spectrometry Imaging (MALDI-MSI) maps molecular distributions directly on tissue sections. It combines mass spectrometry with spatial localization to study metabolites, lipids, or proteins.
Input: Prepared tissue section Output: Spatially resolved mass spectrometry imaging data file.
Tags: MALDI-MSI, MALDI imaging, mass spectrometry imaging, spatial metabolomics, molecular imaging tissue, matrix-assisted laser desorption ionization imaging

Name: Mass Spectrometry
Description:
Mass spectrometry identifies and quantifies molecules based on their mass-to-charge ratios. The assay ionizes chemical compounds and detects their spectral signatures to determine molecular composition.
Input: Prepared chemical or biological sample Output: Mass spectrometry data file containing ion spectra.
Tags: LC-MS, mass spectrometry, MS/MS, tandem mass spec, mass-to-charge measurement, ion detection, mass spec analysis

Name: Mass Spectrometry Analysis
Description: This assay employs mass spectrometry-based analytical workflows to detect, identify, and quantify molecular analytes — such as proteins, peptides, metabolites, or small molecules — from processed biological samples, with acquired spectral data computationally linked to corresponding sample records for integrated downstream analysis. Raw mass spectrometry data files are associated with their respective sample identifiers through a data linkage framework, enabling traceability across sample preparation, instrument acquisition, and bioinformatic processing steps. Analytical outputs, including mass-to-charge ratios, retention times, and ion intensities, are systematically organized to support quantitative comparisons, feature annotation, and statistical interpretation within the broader study context.
Tags: mass spectrometry analysis, MS data processing, protein identification, database search, label-free quantification, spectral analysis

Name: Mass Spectrometry Proteomics
Description:
Mass spectrometry proteomics measures protein abundance and post-translational modifications using peptide fragmentation. The assay generates mass spectra representing peptide fingerprints.
Input: Protein or peptide digest Output: Proteomics mass spectrometry data file.
Tags: proteomics, LC-MS/MS, shotgun proteomics, bottom-up proteomics, peptide mass fingerprinting, tandem MS proteomics

Name: Mass Spectrometry Proteomics Analysis
Description:
Mass spectrometry proteomics analysis processes raw proteomics data to identify and quantify peptides and proteins. It performs database matching and normalization to generate protein-level abundance profiles.
Input: Raw proteomics mass spectrometry data Output: Processed protein quantification tables and annotated identifications.
Tags: proteomics data analysis, MaxQuant, Perseus, protein quantification, proteomics pipeline, peptide identification

Name: Migration Assay Analysis
Description: This assay evaluates the migratory capacity of cells under defined experimental conditions, quantifying movement through or across a barrier (such as a membrane or wound gap) as a measure of cellular motility and invasive potential. Standard methodologies may include scratch/wound-healing assays or Transwell-based migration chambers, with imaging and automated or manual analysis used to quantify migrated cell numbers or closure rates over time. Data are linked and tracked to enable downstream integration with complementary datasets, supporting multi-parameter analyses of cell behavior in the context of disease modeling, drug screening, or mechanistic studies.
Tags: cell migration analysis, scratch assay analysis, wound healing analysis, transwell migration analysis, motility quantification, chemotaxis analysis

Name: Model Creation
Description:
Model creation establishes experimental or computational representations of biological or physical systems. It defines parameters, inputs, and structure for simulation or predictive testing.
Input: Experimental data or design parameters Output: Constructed model or prototype for validation.
Tags: model building, computational model generation, machine learning model training, predictive model development, model architecture

Name: Model Validation
Description: This assay represents a model validation workflow in which experimental data are formally linked to a predictive or computational model to assess its accuracy and reliability. The validation process involves comparing model-predicted outputs against observed experimental measurements, using associated standard operating procedures to ensure consistent sample preparation, instrumentation, and analytical methods. Results are evaluated to confirm that the model meets predefined acceptance criteria for performance, reproducibility, and scientific rigor.
Tags: model validation, model performance evaluation, model accuracy, cross-validation, model testing, generalization assessment

Name: Mouse Challenge
Description:
A mouse challenge assay introduces a defined treatment, infection, or condition to experimental mice. It assesses physiological, immunological, or pathological responses to controlled exposures.
Input: Experimental mouse model Output: Treated or exposed mouse sample for downstream analysis.
Tags: murine challenge, mouse infection model, mouse treatment experiment, in vivo mouse model, mouse exposure

Name: MRI
Description:
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) uses magnetic fields and radio waves to generate detailed internal images of tissues and organs. It provides structural and functional information non-invasively.
Input: Live animal or biological subject Output: MRI image data showing anatomical or functional details.
Tags: MRI, magnetic resonance imaging, structural MRI, tissue imaging, brain MRI, body MRI, NMR-based imaging

Name: MRI Analysis
Description: Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) analysis is a non-invasive imaging technique that utilizes strong magnetic fields and radiofrequency pulses to generate high-resolution, three-dimensional anatomical images of biological tissues and organs. This assay enables quantitative and qualitative assessment of structural morphology, tissue composition, and pathological changes without the use of ionizing radiation. Acquired image data are processed and analyzed using dedicated software to extract measurable parameters such as tissue volume, signal intensity, and structural dimensions, supporting applications in disease characterization, longitudinal monitoring, and preclinical or clinical research studies.
Tags: MRI image analysis, volumetric MRI analysis, tissue segmentation, radiological image analysis, brain volume analysis

Name: Mutational Spectra Analysis
Description: This assay characterizes the mutational spectrum of biological samples by identifying and quantifying the types, frequencies, and patterns of mutations (such as base substitutions, insertions, and deletions) across a defined genomic region or whole genome. The analysis typically employs next-generation sequencing (NGS) or Sanger sequencing technologies, followed by bioinformatic processing to classify mutations by type (e.g., transitions, transversions) and context, enabling the construction of mutational signatures. Data attached to this assay record provide the primary output of the spectral analysis, which may include mutation frequency tables, signature decomposition results, and graphical representations of the mutational landscape. Results are used to assess mutagenic potential, characterize mutagen exposure, or investigate DNA repair mechanisms in the experimental system under study.
Tags: mutation signature analysis, SBS signature, COSMIC signature, trinucleotide context, mutational landscape, mutagen characterization

Name: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
Description:
Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy measures the magnetic properties of atomic nuclei to determine molecular structures. It provides detailed chemical composition and conformational data.
Input: Prepared chemical or biological sample Output: NMR spectral data file representing molecular structure information.
Tags: NMR spectroscopy, proton NMR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR, metabolomics NMR, chemical structure NMR, magnetic resonance spectroscopy

Name: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy Analysis
Description: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectroscopy is employed to characterize the structural and chemical properties of biological samples by detecting the resonance frequencies of atomic nuclei (typically ¹H, ¹³C, or other NMR-active nuclei) in a magnetic field. This technique enables the identification, quantification, and structural elucidation of metabolites, small molecules, or macromolecular components within a sample, providing detailed information on molecular identity and concentration. Data generated from NMR acquisitions are linked to associated experimental records, facilitating integrated analysis and traceability of spectral outputs across the study dataset.
Tags: NMR analysis, NMR data processing, spectral assignment, metabolite identification NMR, peak assignment, NMR peak analysis

Name: Oncopanel
Description:
An oncopanel assay sequences a targeted set of cancer-associated genes to detect somatic mutations. The process involves library preparation, sequencing, and variant calling for genomic profiling.
Input: DNA sample Output: Oncopanel sequencing file (e.g., FASTQ / D.SEQ) containing targeted read data.
Tags: cancer gene panel, targeted oncology sequencing, tumor mutation profiling, cancer genomics, targeted NGS oncology, somatic variant detection

Name: Patient Visit
Description: This assay captures administrative and clinical metadata associated with patient study visits, serving as a structured data collection framework rather than a biological measurement assay. It records visit-level contextual information such as visit dates, timepoints, subject identifiers, clinical site details, and protocol-relevant annotations that are essential for linking experimental samples and assay results to their proper clinical context. This metadata layer ensures traceability and data integrity across all associated biospecimen collections and downstream analytical assays within the study.
Tags: clinical visit, study visit, patient encounter, blood draw visit, sample collection visit, longitudinal visit, study timepoint

Name: PDX Cell Line Generation
Description:
PDX (Patient-Derived Xenograft) cell line generation establishes tumor cell cultures from implanted patient tissue in animal models. The process isolates and expands cells for drug testing or molecular analysis.
Input: Patient-derived xenograft tissue Output: Cultured PDX cell line suitable for in vitro experimentation.
Tags: PDX cell line, patient-derived xenograft culture, tumor model cell line, xenograft in vitro model

Name: Pendant Drop Tensiometry
Description:
Pendant drop tensiometry measures surface tension by analyzing the shape of a suspended liquid droplet. The assay determines interfacial properties relevant to material or biological systems.
Input: Liquid or solution sample Output: Surface tension data or interfacial tension profile.
Tags: pendant drop tensiometry, surface tension measurement, interfacial tension, droplet shape analysis, tensiometry, surfactant characterization

Name: Permeability Analysis
Description: This assay evaluates the permeability characteristics of biological membranes, barriers, or compound formulations, measuring the rate and extent of molecular transport across a defined membrane system. The analysis is conducted using standardized permeability protocols, with experimental data linked to associated datasets for integrated analysis and cross-referencing. Results typically quantify flux, permeability coefficients, or transport rates to assess barrier integrity or compound penetration potential. This linked-data format enables traceability between raw measurements and downstream analytical outputs, supporting reproducibility and data integrity in permeability assessments.
Tags: barrier permeability analysis, membrane transport analysis, TEER measurement, permeability coefficient, paracellular flux, transwell permeability

Name: PET/CT Scan
Description:
A PET/CT scan combines positron emission tomography with computed tomography to visualize metabolic activity and anatomical structure. It provides functional imaging for research or diagnostic applications.
Input: Live subject or biological model Output: PET/CT imaging data showing metabolic and anatomical information.
Tags: PET-CT, positron emission tomography, CT scan, nuclear imaging, FDG-PET, metabolic imaging, hybrid imaging

Name: PET/CT Scan Analysis
Description: Positron emission tomography combined with computed tomography (PET/CT) is a dual-modality nuclear imaging assay that simultaneously quantifies metabolic activity and anatomical structure by detecting gamma rays emitted from a positron-emitting radiolabeled tracer (typically 18F-FDG) administered to the subject. The PET component provides functional imaging of tracer uptake reflecting cellular glucose metabolism, while the CT component supplies high-resolution structural context for precise anatomical localization and attenuation correction. Standardized uptake values (SUVs) and volumetric parameters are derived from reconstructed images to assess lesion burden, disease activity, or treatment response across tissues of interest.
Tags: PET-CT analysis, nuclear imaging analysis, FDG uptake analysis, volumetric PET analysis, SUV analysis, metabolic imaging analysis

Name: Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)
Description:
PCR amplifies specific DNA sequences to generate measurable quantities for downstream analysis. It cycles through denaturation, annealing, and extension to exponentially replicate target regions.
Input: DNA sample and primers Output: Amplified DNA product suitable for quantification or sequencing.
Tags: PCR, qPCR, RT-PCR, gene amplification, primer-based amplification, DNA amplification, quantitative PCR

Name: Protein Extraction
Description:
Protein extraction isolates total or specific proteins from cells, tissues, or lysates. It uses buffers and mechanical or chemical disruption to obtain soluble proteins.
Input: Biological material Output: Purified protein extract for downstream assays.
Tags: protein isolation, protein purification, cell protein extraction, tissue protein extraction, total protein extraction

Name: Proximity Extension Assay
Description:
A proximity extension assay (PEA) quantifies protein abundance using antibody-linked DNA probes. When probes bind to target proteins, they hybridize and are amplified for detection.
Input: Biological sample Output: Quantitative protein detection data file.
Tags: Olink assay, proximity extension, proximity ligation, protein biomarker panel, PEA, high-sensitivity proteomics panel

Name: RaDR Image Machine Learning Analysis
Description:
RaDR image machine learning analysis applies trained computational models to interpret fluorescence or microscopy data from DNA damage assays. It automates quantification of damage signals and cell-level responses.
Input: Raw RaDR assay imaging data Output: Processed machine learning–derived metrics and classification results.
Tags: RaDR image ML, machine learning image analysis, automated fluorescence analysis, deep learning microscopy analysis

Name: Sample Processing
Description:
Sample processing prepares biological or environmental materials for downstream assays. It includes aliquoting, labeling, and quality control to ensure data integrity.
Input: Collected sample Output: Processed aliquot or prepared material for subsequent analysis.
Tags: sample preparation, biological sample processing, pre-analytical processing, sample handling, sample prep protocol

Name: Sensor Creation
Description:
Sensor creation involves designing and assembling devices that detect physical, chemical, or biological signals. It combines materials engineering and functionalization to produce a responsive detection unit.
Input: Design materials and sensor components Output: Fabricated sensor prototype ready for calibration or testing.
Tags: biosensor fabrication, sensor development, electrochemical sensor construction, device fabrication, sensor manufacturing

Name: Short Read Sequencing
Description: Short read sequencing is a high-throughput next-generation sequencing (NGS) approach used to determine the nucleotide sequence of DNA or RNA fragments, enabling detection of genetic variants, gene expression profiles, or genomic features depending on the library preparation strategy employed. This assay involves fragmentation of input nucleic acids, adapter ligation, and amplification prior to sequencing on short-read platforms (such as Illumina), generating millions of short sequence reads (typically 50–300 bp) in parallel. The resulting raw sequencing data are linked to associated metadata and downstream bioinformatic analyses, including quality control, alignment to a reference genome or transcriptome, and quantification or variant calling as appropriate to the experimental design.
Tags: Illumina sequencing, short-read NGS, paired-end sequencing, next-generation sequencing, 150bp reads, sequencing run, HiSeq NovaSeq

Name: Simulation Data
Description:
Simulation data generation uses computational models to recreate experimental conditions or predict outcomes. It provides synthetic datasets for validation, hypothesis testing, or algorithm development.
Input: Simulation parameters or model inputs Output: Simulated dataset representing modeled system behavior.
Tags: computational simulation, in silico data, molecular dynamics output, theoretical calculation, quantum chemistry output

Name: Single Cell Clustering Analysis
Description:
Single cell clustering analysis organizes single-cell expression data into groups with shared transcriptional profiles. It applies dimensional reduction and clustering algorithms to identify cellular subpopulations.
Input: Single-cell expression matrix Output: Clustered dataset and annotated cell-type identities.
Tags: scRNA clustering, Seurat clustering, Leiden clustering, cell type annotation, UMAP clustering, dimensionality reduction clustering

Name: Single Cell Expression Matrix Analysis
Description:
Single cell expression matrix analysis processes sequencing data to generate normalized per-cell expression profiles. It integrates quality control, normalization, and dimensionality reduction.
Input: Single-cell sequencing data Output: Processed expression matrix with cell-level transcript quantifications.
Tags: single cell expression matrix, gene expression count matrix, feature-barcode matrix, scRNA expression data, single cell count table
Alternative Assay Names: Single Cell Expression Analysis, Single Nuclear RNASequencing Expression Analysis

Name: Slide Imaging
Description:
Slide imaging digitizes microscope slides to capture high-resolution representations of tissue or cell samples. It enables archiving and computational image analysis.
Input: Prepared slide Output: Digital slide image file for visualization or analysis.
Tags: tissue slide imaging, glass slide scanning, digital slide scan, whole slide imaging, WSI scan, histology slide imaging

Name: Spatial Metabolomics
Description: Spatial metabolomics is an analytical approach that maps the distribution and relative abundance of small molecules, lipids, and other metabolites directly within tissue sections or biological samples, preserving spatial context that is lost in conventional bulk metabolomics. This technique typically employs mass spectrometry-based imaging methods such as MALDI-MSI (Matrix-Assisted Laser Desorption/Ionization Mass Spectrometry Imaging), DESI-MSI (Desorption Electrospray Ionization), or related platforms, applied to cryosectioned or fresh-frozen tissue mounted on conductive slides. Sample preparation generally involves careful sectioning, matrix application (for MALDI-based workflows), and instrument acquisition across a defined spatial grid, followed by data processing to generate ion intensity maps correlated with tissue morphology. Downstream analysis integrates mass spectral identification of metabolite features with histological annotation, enabling spatially resolved profiling of metabolic states across distinct tissue regions, cell types, or microenvironments.
Tags: spatial metabolomics, MALDI metabolite imaging, MSI metabolomics, molecular metabolite mapping, tissue metabolite distribution

Name: Spatial Proteomics
Description: Spatial proteomics is a set of techniques that enables the measurement and localization of proteins within their native tissue or cellular context, providing both quantitative protein abundance data and spatial distribution information at subcellular, cellular, or tissue-wide resolution. These methods typically employ imaging mass spectrometry (e.g., MALDI-MSI or DESI-MSI), multiplexed immunofluorescence or immunohistochemistry, or mass cytometry-based imaging (e.g., IMC or MIBI) to detect and map hundreds to thousands of proteins simultaneously across tissue sections. Sample preparation generally involves fresh-frozen or FFPE tissue sectioning, antigen retrieval, and labeling with isotope- or fluorophore-conjugated antibodies, followed by high-resolution imaging and computational image analysis pipelines for protein quantification and spatial mapping. The resulting data enable characterization of tissue architecture, cell-type composition, protein co-localization, and microenvironmental interactions in biological and clinical specimens.
Tags: spatial proteomics, tissue protein mapping, multiplexed protein imaging, DSP, IMC proteomics, GeoMx, CyCIF proteomics

Name: Spatial Transcriptomics Analysis
Description:
Spatial transcriptomics analysis processes sequencing and positional data to reconstruct gene expression patterns across tissue space. It integrates imaging and transcriptomic profiles.
Input: Raw spatial transcriptomics data Output: Processed spatial expression maps and annotated gene matrices.
Tags: spatial gene expression analysis, Visium analysis, spatially resolved analysis, in situ transcriptomics analysis, tissue transcriptomic mapping

Name: Spectroscopy
Description: Chemical spectroscopy encompasses a broad suite of analytical techniques that measure the interaction of electromagnetic radiation with matter to determine the composition, structure, and concentration of chemical species within a sample. Methods may include UV-Vis, infrared (IR), Raman, nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), mass spectrometry-coupled approaches, and atomic spectroscopy, each employing distinct wavelength ranges and instrumentation tailored to specific analyte properties. Sample preparation varies by technique but typically involves dissolution, dilution, or direct presentation of the sample to the instrument, followed by spectral acquisition and quantitative or qualitative analysis using reference standards or spectral libraries. Collectively, these methods provide highly sensitive and selective characterization of molecular identity, purity, and abundance across a wide range of biological and chemical matrices.
Tags: spectroscopy, optical measurement, wavelength-based analysis, light interaction, spectrophotometry, molecular spectroscopy

Name: Spectroscopy Analysis
Description: Spectroscopy analysis encompasses the measurement of the interaction between matter and electromagnetic radiation across defined wavelength ranges to characterize the physical, chemical, or molecular properties of biological samples. Raw spectroscopic data are collected using appropriate instrumentation (e.g., UV-Vis, IR, NMR, or mass spectrometry platforms) and subsequently processed to extract quantitative or qualitative information such as absorbance, emission intensity, or spectral signatures. The resulting data support applications including compound identification, concentration determination, and structural characterization within a biomedical research context.
Tags: spectral analysis, peak analysis, wavelength intensity analysis, absorption emission analysis, spectroscopy data processing

Name: SureQuant Analysis
Description:
SureQuant analysis processes targeted mass spectrometry data to quantify peptides or proteins using internal standards. It calculates absolute or relative abundance values from multiple reaction monitoring (MRM) datasets.
Input: Raw SureQuant mass spectrometry data Output: Processed quantitative protein measurements and validation metrics.
Tags: SureQuant, targeted proteomics, heavy-labeled standard peptide, IS-PRM, triggered data-dependent acquisition, internal standard quantification, Orbitrap targeted MS

Name: Tissue Imaging
Description: Tissue Imaging – Metadata refers to the systematic collection and standardization of descriptive information associated with tissue imaging experiments, capturing key parameters such as sample provenance, preparation protocols, staining or labeling methods, instrument settings, and image acquisition conditions. This metadata framework ensures reproducibility and interoperability of tissue imaging data by documenting contextual details including tissue type, fixation and sectioning procedures, imaging modality (e.g., fluorescence microscopy, brightfield, or multiplexed imaging), and analytical pipeline specifications. Standardized metadata records enable downstream integration, searchability, and quality control of imaging datasets across studies and platforms.
Tags: tissue microscopy, tissue slide imaging, fluorescence tissue imaging, histological imaging, tissue staining imaging, whole tissue imaging

Name: Tissue Sectioning
Description: Tissue sectioning is a histological preparation method in which fixed or frozen biological tissue samples are physically cut into thin slices (typically ranging from 1 to 10 micrometers for paraffin-embedded or cryosectioned material) using a microtome or cryostat instrument. This process preserves the spatial architecture and cellular morphology of the tissue, enabling downstream analyses such as immunohistochemistry, immunofluorescence, in situ hybridization, or routine histological staining. Sections are mounted onto glass slides and may undergo additional processing steps such as deparaffinization, antigen retrieval, or permeabilization prior to labeling and imaging-based readout.
Tags: cryosectioning, microtome sectioning, FFPE sectioning, tissue slicing, cryo-section preparation, histology sectioning

Name: TransCompR Cross Species Modeling
Description:
TransCompR cross-species modeling integrates datasets across species to predict conserved biological relationships. It applies computational models to align gene or protein networks.
Input: Cross-species expression or phenotype datasets Output: Modeled comparative relationships and prediction outputs.
Tags: TransCompR, cross-species modeling, comparative computational modeling, species translation model, cross-species computational

Name: Tumoroid Formation
Description:
Tumoroid formation generates 3D tumor-like structures from cancer cells or tissue fragments. The assay models tumor architecture and microenvironment for drug testing or imaging.
Input: Cancer cells or tumor fragments Output: 3D tumoroid culture ready for analysis.
Tags: tumoroid, tumor organoid, 3D tumor model, cancer organoid, tumor spheroid, patient-derived organoid, tumor 3D culture

Name: Western Blot
Description: Western blot is a widely used immunodetection technique that separates proteins by molecular weight via gel electrophoresis, transfers them onto a membrane (typically nitrocellulose or PVDF), and detects specific target proteins using primary and secondary antibodies conjugated to reporter molecules such as horseradish peroxidase or fluorescent dyes. Sample preparation typically involves cell or tissue lysis, protein quantification, and denaturation in SDS-containing loading buffer prior to electrophoresis. Detection is commonly achieved through chemiluminescent or fluorescent imaging systems, with band intensity analyzed using densitometry software to enable semi-quantitative assessment of protein expression levels. This assay is routinely employed to characterize protein abundance, post-translational modifications, and molecular weight confirmation across a wide range of biological samples.
Tags: western blot, immunoblot, SDS-PAGE, protein gel, WB, blotting, nitrocellulose membrane, protein band detection

Name: X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy
Description: X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS) is a surface-sensitive analytical technique that measures the elemental composition, chemical state, and electronic structure of materials by irradiating a sample surface with X-rays and detecting the kinetic energy and number of photoelectrons emitted from the top 1–10 nm of the material. This assay provides quantitative information on atomic concentrations and chemical bonding environments of elements present at or near the sample surface, enabling characterization of thin films, coatings, nanoparticles, and biomaterial interfaces. Data are typically acquired under ultra-high vacuum conditions using a monochromatic X-ray source, with spectral analysis performed by binding energy peak identification, curve fitting, and comparison to reference databases to determine elemental and chemical speciation.
Tags: XPS, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, surface analysis, elemental composition, binding energy measurement, ESCA, surface chemistry

Name: Long-Read Sequencing
Description: Long read sequencing is a next-generation sequencing approach that generates nucleic acid sequence reads typically exceeding 10 kilobases in length, enabling the detection of complex genomic features such as structural variants, repetitive elements, full-length transcript isoforms, and phased haplotypes that are difficult to resolve with short-read technologies. This methodology employs platforms such as Pacific Biosciences (PacBio) Single Molecule Real-Time (SMRT) sequencing or Oxford Nanopore Technologies (ONT) sequencing, which capture sequence information from single DNA or RNA molecules in real time. Sample preparation generally involves high-molecular-weight nucleic acid extraction, library construction with minimal fragmentation, and size selection to preserve long fragments prior to sequencing. Resulting data are analyzed using specialized bioinformatic pipelines for base calling, alignment to reference genomes, and downstream variant or isoform detection, providing comprehensive insights into genome structure, epigenetic modifications, and transcriptome complexity.
Tags: long-read sequencing, Oxford Nanopore, PacBio SMRT, third-generation sequencing, long reads, nanopore sequencing

Name: Fluorescence-Activated Cell Sorting (FACS)
Description: Fluorescence-Activated Cell Sorting (FACS) is a flow cytometry-based technique used to detect, quantify, and physically isolate distinct cell populations based on fluorescent marker expression and light-scattering properties. Cells labeled with fluorescently conjugated antibodies or reporter molecules are passed individually through a laser beam, enabling simultaneous multiparametric analysis of surface and intracellular markers. Sorted cell populations are collected for downstream applications such as genomic, transcriptomic, or functional analyses, with data linked to associated experimental records for integrated analysis.
Tags: FACS, fluorescence-activated cell sorting, cell sorting, flow-based separation, live cell sorting, electrostatic cell deflection

Name: Microvascular Network Formation Assay
Description: This assay characterizes the formation and morphological properties of microvascular networks, capturing key metadata parameters associated with the self-organization of endothelial or vascular cells into tubular, network-like structures in vitro. Quantitative endpoints typically include measurements of total tube length, branch points, network complexity, mesh area, and vessel segment characteristics, derived through image analysis of cells cultured under pro-angiogenic conditions. The assay serves as a functional readout of angiogenic capacity and vascular morphogenesis, supporting studies of pro- and anti-angiogenic compounds, extracellular matrix interactions, or cell co-culture systems.
Tags: microvascular network, vascular chip assay, angiogenesis chip, microvascular formation, endothelial network, vascular OOC

Name: Organ-on-a-Chip Device Fabrication
Description: This assay encompasses the fabrication and assembly of a microfluidic Organ-on-a-Chip (OOC) device engineered from cell-embedded layers (CELs) to recapitulate physiologically relevant tissue microenvironments for cancer cell experimentation. The fabrication process involves the integration of living cellular components into microfluidic architectures designed to mimic in vivo organ-level structure and function, enabling controlled perfusion, cell-cell interactions, and spatiotemporal manipulation of the tumor microenvironment. The resulting platform serves as an advanced in vitro model for investigating cancer cell behavior, drug responses, and tumor-stromal interactions under dynamic, physiologically representative conditions.
Tags: OOC fabrication, organ chip creation, microfluidic organ chip, tissue chip fabrication, body-on-chip, organ-on-chip assembly

Name: Cell Culture
Description: This assay captures standardized metadata associated with cell culture experimental conditions, documenting key parameters such as cell line identity, passage number, growth media composition, seeding density, incubation conditions (temperature, CO₂ levels, humidity), and treatment details. The recorded metadata serves as essential contextual information to ensure reproducibility and traceability of downstream biological assays performed on cultured cells. This structured documentation supports data integration, quality control, and cross-experiment comparisons within biomedical research workflows.
Tags: in vitro culture, cell growth, cell propagation, cell maintenance, tissue culture, cell expansion, cell passage

Name: Cell Extraction
Description: This assay captures and standardizes the metadata associated with cell extraction procedures, documenting key experimental parameters such as cell type, passage number, extraction method, reagents used, and processing conditions applied during the preparation of cellular material for downstream analyses. It serves as a critical data governance step to ensure traceability, reproducibility, and quality control across experiments by recording provenance information for extracted cell samples. The structured metadata collected supports integration with subsequent assays and facilitates cross-study comparisons within biomedical research workflows.
Tags: cell isolation, cell dissociation, cell extraction, tissue dissociation, enzymatic dissociation, single cell suspension

Name: Cell Isolation
Description: This assay captures metadata associated with cell isolation procedures, documenting the methodological parameters, reagents, instruments, and conditions used to separate and purify specific cell populations from biological samples. It serves as a standardized record of the isolation workflow, including details such as tissue source, dissociation methods, separation techniques (e.g., density gradient centrifugation, fluorescence- or magnetic-activated cell sorting), and quality metrics applied to ensure cellular integrity and purity. The metadata provides essential provenance and reproducibility information to contextualize downstream analyses performed on the isolated cell populations.
Tags: cell isolation, cell sorting, cell separation, cell purification, immunomagnetic isolation, negative selection, positive selection

Name: Bacterial Extraction
Description: This assay captures metadata associated with bacterial extraction procedures, documenting the experimental parameters, sample identifiers, reagents, and procedural conditions used during the isolation and extraction of bacterial material from biological or environmental samples. It serves as a standardized record-keeping component that accompanies downstream analytical assays, ensuring traceability and reproducibility by cataloging key variables such as sample source, extraction method, instrument settings, and processing dates. The metadata framework supports data integration, quality control, and cross-study comparisons by providing structured contextual information linked to each bacterial extraction event.
Tags: bacterial isolation, bacteria extraction, microbial extraction, pathogen isolation, bacterial culture isolation

Name: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
Description: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectroscopy is employed to characterize the structural, chemical, and physical properties of molecules by detecting the resonance frequencies of atomic nuclei (typically ¹H, ¹³C, or other NMR-active nuclei) in a strong magnetic field. This assay provides quantitative and qualitative information on molecular identity, purity, conformation, and dynamics, and may be applied to small molecules, metabolites, proteins, or other biomolecules in solution or solid state. Data generated are linked to associated experimental records, enabling integrated analysis and traceability of spectral outputs, chemical shift assignments, and derived structural parameters within the broader research dataset.
Tags: NMR spectroscopy, proton NMR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR, metabolomics NMR, chemical structure NMR, magnetic resonance spectroscopy
